pr.title: lb: Match audio, snap, and refract functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Attempted to match several lb module functions across audio, snapshot, THP/movie texture handling, background flash, card, and refract utilities. The PR was closed unmerged as a duplicate of #2037 because #2037 had already been reviewed and, according to the closing comment, properly reverted the fn_800204C8 partial match and lb_8001C4A8 variable reordering that caused regressions. Useful evidence remains for matching tactics: bool return correction for lbAudioAx_800265C4, targeted M2C_FIELD use for unknown HSD_CObj/HSD_GObj fields, GX texture object setup for lbMthp, and state-machine reconstruction in lbbgflash, but the bgflash partial and card variable reorder should be treated as suspect.
what_changed: Implemented fn_800263B4 as a void helper that frees a non-null pointer through HSD_ObjFree(&lbl_80433710, ptr), and updated its header prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void fn_800263B4(void* ptr).

Implemented lbAudioAx_800265C4 as a bool-returning search over HSD_GObj entities, using M2C_FIELD(HSD_GObj_Entities, HSD_GObj**, 0xF8), checking gobj->user_data fields, keying off a matching sfx id with AXDriverKeyOff, unlinking the gobj through HSD_GObjPLink_80390228, and returning true/false. Header changed the return type from enum_t to bool and the sfx parameter to int.

Added a local BgFlashData2 struct in lbbgflash.c and implemented fn_800204C8 as a state/mode dispatcher over lbl_80433658, calling fn_8001FC08 for modes 0-2 and updating xC-xF from float fields, and handling modes 3-4 with x/y stepping and terminal flag x33. This was described in the PR text as only 91.9% matched and later identified in the closing comment as a partial match reverted in #2037.

Reordered local declarations in lb_8001C4A8 and split an inline anonymous struct declaration into separate unk0/unk4/unk8 fields. The closing comment says this variable reordering caused regressions and was reverted in #2037.

Implemented lbMthp8001F890 to initialize fields in static lbl_804335B8, create an SObj through HSD_SObjLib_803A477C, set bit 0x10 in the returned object's x40 field, and return the object pointer. Header return type changed from UNK_T to void*.
Implemented lbMthp8001F928 to initialize and load three GXTexObj textures: one full-size texture and two half-size textures, followed by HSD_SObjLib_803A49E0(gobj, arg1).

Expanded lbmthp.static.h with GXStruct include, an HSD_SObj_803A477C_t_ext overlay, a lbl_804335B8_t static layout containing three GXTexObj fields, image pointers, width/height fields, SObj setup fields, STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof == 0xA0), and static declarations for lbl_804335B8 and lbl_804D7CE0.

Implemented lbRefract_8002247C as a projection-type switch using HSD_CObjGetProjectionType and MTXLightPerspective, MTXLightFrustum, or MTXLightOrtho to write a matrix at lbl_804336D0 + 0x10. It used M2C_FIELD reads from HSD_CObj offsets such as 0x38, 0x40, 0x44, 0x48, and 0x4c.

Adjusted lbSnap_8001DF6C to introduce a ret local, preserve ptr->unk0 before branching, assign ret from lb_8001BB48 when work is performed, and return ret at the end. PR summary claimed this matched lbSnap_8001DF6C 100%.

Included additional headers required by the implementations, including m2c_macros.h, baselib/gobjplink.h, dolphin/gx/GXTexture.h, dolphin/gx/GXStruct.h, sysdolphin/baselib/sobjlib.h, and baselib/cobj.h.
smart_moves: Changed lbAudioAx_800265C4 from enum_t to bool after review feedback, matching the observed true/false behavior instead of preserving a vague decompiler placeholder type.
Used M2C_FIELD for unresolved structure offsets in HSD_GObj_Entities and HSD_CObj instead of inventing premature full struct definitions; this keeps field-offset evidence explicit and searchable.
Added STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct lbl_804335B8_t) == 0xA0) when introducing a new static data layout, which is a good guardrail for decomp struct recovery.
Kept lbMthp texture setup close to GX call semantics: initialize texture object, initialize LOD, load the texture object, and repeat with the correct full-size and half-size dimensions.
For lbSnap_8001DF6C, the ret local unified the return path and avoided returning directly from the call expression, which can help match compiler register/lifetime behavior.
decomp_lessons: A match percentage or local codegen success is not enough if the change causes behavioral or project regressions; the closing comment explicitly flags fn_800204C8 and lb_8001C4A8 as reverted in the reviewed duplicate PR.

Partial matches such as fn_800204C8 at 91.9% should be isolated and reviewed carefully before landing, especially when they reconstruct state-machine control flow with gotos and inferred overlay structs.
Variable declaration reordering in seemingly minor cleanup can matter for matching or regressions; avoid landing declaration/order churn unless it is required and verified.
Prefer specific prototypes once behavior is clear: void for free helpers, bool for true/false search-and-action functions, and void* only when the real object type is not yet safely known.
When accessing unknown HSD object internals, M2C_FIELD can be an acceptable temporary tactic, but it should signal that a future struct/header cleanup may be needed.
Static data overlays should include exact offsets, comments, and STATIC_ASSERTs so future decomp work can refine names without losing layout guarantees.
naming_conventions: Library functions in this slice retain existing lb-prefixed names such as lbAudioAx_800265C4, lbBgFlash_800205F0, lbMthp8001F890, lbRefract_8002247C, and lbSnap_8001DF6C.
Unknown internal functions remain address-style fn_800xxxxx names, e.g. fn_800263B4 and fn_800204C8, until a semantic name is justified.
Static/global labels keep lbl_ address naming such as lbl_80433710, lbl_80433658, lbl_804335B8, lbl_804D7CE0, and lbl_804336D0.
Temporary/inferred structs used descriptive-but-provisional names such as BgFlashData2, HSD_SObj_803A477C_t_ext, and lbl_804335B8_t.
Struct fields remain offset-style xNN/unk_NN where semantics are uncertain, with occasional comments like width, height, image ptr, or texture number when evidence is clearer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use PAD_STACK where stack padding is needed to reproduce codegen, shown in lbAudioAx_800265C4 and lbSnap_8001DF6C.
Use M2C_FIELD for exact-offset field access when the complete type is not yet modeled, shown for HSD_GObj_Entities at 0xF8 and HSD_CObj projection fields at 0x38/0x40/0x44/0x48/0x4c.
Introduce local pointer aliases such as pWidth and pHeight in lbMthp8001F928 to influence loads and casts for half-size texture dimensions.
Use explicit casts to u16 for shifted texture dimensions and to u32/s32 in comparisons/field assignments where the decompiled code requires matching signedness or width.
State-machine reconstruction in fn_800204C8 used labels/gotos for case blocks, but this specific implementation should be treated as risky because it was later called out as a reverted partial match.
Unified return variable pattern in lbSnap_8001DF6C likely helped match control flow and return register behavior.
review_feedback: PR text says review feedback was addressed with M2C_FIELD and a bool return type.
Closing comment: the PR was closed as a duplicate of #2037.
Closing comment: #2037 had already been reviewed and properly reverted the fn_800204C8 partial match and lb_8001C4A8 variable reordering that caused regressions.
No line-level review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Compare PR #2037 against this PR to identify the exact reverted fn_800204C8 and lb_8001C4A8 changes and the accepted replacements.
Audit whether lbAudioAx_800265C4's bool prototype and M2C_FIELD usage landed via #2037 or a later PR.
Search later lbmthp.static.h history for refinements to lbl_804335B8_t and HSD_SObj_803A477C_t_ext field names.
Search for later struct definitions that replace M2C_FIELD accesses in lbRefract_8002247C with named HSD_CObj fields.
Check whether fn_800204C8 was eventually fully matched and whether BgFlashData2 survived or was renamed/refined.
